channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Zero-Day That Lingered for Months
item.description: An attacker has been using maliciously crafted PDF files to exploit a zero-day in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for at least four months....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Stolen Rockstar Games analytics data leaked by extortion gang
item.description: Rockstar Games has suffered a data breach linked to a recent security incident at Anodot, with the ShinyHunters extortion gang now leaking the stolen data on its data leak site. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical flaw in wolfSSL library enables forged certificate use
item.description: A critical vulnerability in the wolfSSL SSL/TLS library can weaken security via improper verification of the hash algorithm or its size when checking Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA)...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Empty Attestations: OT Lacks the Tools for Cryptographic Readiness
item.description: OT asset owners are being asked by regulators to attest to their post-quantum cryptographic readiness without the appropriate tooling, resulting in paperwork dressed up to look like genuine security....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI takedown of W3LL phishing service leads to developer arrest
item.description: The FBI Atlanta Field Office and Indonesian authorities have dismantled the "W3LL" global phishing platform, seizing infrastructure and arresting the alleged developer in what is described as the firs...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: OpenAI rotates macOS certs after Axios attack hit code-signing workflow
item.description: OpenAI is rotating potentially exposed macOS code-signing certificates after a GitHub Actions workflow executed a malicious Axios package during a recent supply chain attack. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Booking.com data breach forces reservation PIN resets
item.description: Booking.com has confirmed via a statement to BleepingComputer that it has detected unauthorized access to its systems that has exposed sensitive reservation and user data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
item.description: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. A modified version of BX RAT, JanelaRAT is kno...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Adobe rolls out emergency fix for Acrobat, Reader zero-day flaw
item.description: Adobe has released an emergency security update for Acrobat Reader to fix a vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-34621, that has been exploited in zero-day attacks since at least December. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
item.description: The prolific China-backed threat group is targeting AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba cloud environments and using typosquatting to obscure C2 communication....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an off-...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The silent “Storm”: New infostealer hijacks sessions, decrypts server-side
item.description: New "Storm" infostealer skips local decryption, sending browser data to attacker servers. Varonis shows how server-side decryption enables session hijacking, bypassing passwords and MFA. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
item.description: Monday is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive state-s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
item.description: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi Whitmo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
item.description: The North Korean hacking group tracked as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and added ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
item.description: OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised. "O...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical Marimo pre-auth RCE flaw now under active exploitation
item.description: A critical pre-authentication remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Marimo is now under active exploitation, leveraged for credential theft. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve m...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
item.description: Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-3462...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Over 20,000 crypto fraud victims identified in international crackdown
item.description: An international law enforcement action led by the U.K.'s National Crime Agency (NCA) has identified over 20,000 victims of cryptocurrency fraud across Canada, the United Kingdom, and the United State...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
item.description: Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global geolocation ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ChatGPT rolls out new $100 Pro subscription to challenge Claude
item.description: OpenAI has rolled out a new Pro subscription that costs $100 and is in line with Claude's pricing, which also has a $100 subscription, in addition to the $200 Max monthly plan. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hims Breach Exposes the Most Sensitive Kinds of PHI
item.description: Threat actors breached the telehealth brand, and now they may know patients' personal health details. What could they do with that information?...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
item.description: These are the fundamental detection model shifts cybersecurity teams need to make to keep up with the rising number of credential-based attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Nearly 4,000 US industrial devices exposed to Iranian cyberattacks
item.description: The attack surface targeted by Iranian-linked hackers in cyberattacks against U.S. critical infrastructure networks includes thousands of Internet-exposed programmable logic controllers (PLCs) manufac...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center to Combat Cybersecurity and Fraud Threats
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Orange Business Reimagines Enterprise Voice Communications With Trust and AI
item.description: No summary available
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Analysis of one billion CISA KEV remediation records exposes limits of human-scale security
item.description: Analysis of 1 billion CISA KEV remediation records reveal a breaking point for human-scale security. Qualys shows most critical flaws are exploited before defenders can patch them. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Industrial Controllers Still Vulnerable As Conflicts Move to Cyber
item.description: The US government warns programmable logic controllers are being targeted, and research turns up 179 vulnerable operational technology (OT) devices....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the ongoing GlassWorm campaign, which employs a new Zig dropper that's designed to stealthily infect all integrated development environm...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
